reply:
The Perseid Meteor Shower is one of the best stargazing events of the year! You can expect to see up to 100 meteors per hour at peak — that's roughly one every 36 seconds. McDonald Observatory is an excellent choice: Bortle class 2 skies mean almost no light pollution.

The best viewing is after midnight when the radiant (in Perseus) climbs higher. Bring a reclining chair, warm layers, and give your eyes 20 minutes to dark adapt.

[ACTION:view_spot:McDonald Observatory]
